reasoning: Q6 exhibits a 53% performance gap vs GenDB target (29.3 ms vs 14.19-19 ms) with clear CPU-bound bottlenecks: missing SIMD vectorization (20-35% potential) and unused zone maps (15-40% potential). Query is correct and already well-parallelized. With 2 iterations remaining and concrete high-impact optimizations identified, aggressive optimization is justified.
focus_areas: SIMD Vectorization (AVX-512) for predicate evaluation and horizontal reduction
Zone Map Skipping to eliminate scanning blocks outside filter range
strategy_notes: Execute P1 and P2 in parallel: (1) Implement AVX-512 predicate evaluation on lines 118-120 with proper alignment and tail handling; (2) Load and utilize zone map metadata for l_shipdate and l_discount to skip blocks. These are complementary optimizations with distinct code paths. Combined expected improvement: 25-50% (7-15 ms). After this iteration, reassess trajectory and consider P3 (predicate reordering) if P1/P2 alone don't close the gap sufficiently.
notes: High confidence in both recommendations. SIMD is the highest-impact and most well-scoped. Zone maps availability is confirmed in storage_design.json. Recommend implementing SIMD first (architectural impact on inner loop), then zone maps (metadata loading). Verify AVX-512 compiler support (-mavx512f -mavx512dq flags). Use 2 of 3 recommendations for medium-complexity multi-file changes.
